_comment: A REAL source as the ingest pipeline would emit it: Zhuang et al. 2021 (PLOS Medicine), NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study, 521,120 participants — egg/cholesterol intake positively associated with mortality. Argues 'Increases risk' and rests on a genuinely new, independent cohort (NIH-AARP), so adding it should strengthen the minority 'Increases risk' camp with independent evidence (concentration stays low). Verify: https://journals.plos.org/plosmedicine/article?id=10.1371/journal.pmed.1003508

source.title: Zhuang et al. — Egg & cholesterol consumption and mortality (NIH-AARP)

position.quote: each additional half a whole egg/day was associated with a 7% higher risk of all-cause mortality... each 300 mg/day of dietary cholesterol with a 19% higher risk

factorWeights.factor: Judge eggs on biomarkers (LDL) vs hard CVD events
factorWeights.quote: positive associations with cardiovascular, cancer, and all-cause mortality
factorWeights.rationale: Weights hard mortality endpoints from a very large cohort.
